entry.title: Meet Your Ops Agents - Powered by SAP Automation Pilot and Joule
entry.content:
Building on our latest innovation around SAP Automation Pilot and AI-powered IT Operations, I am excited to share that we are already seeing real productive use cases for Day-2 Operations Agents which are powered by SAP Automation Pilot content and consumed directly in Joule in a truly agentic manner.

🎥  Meet Your Ops Agent

See an example about Ops Agent powered by SAP Automation Pilot and discover how easy it is to set up an MCP server, expose automations, and trigger them in an agentic way through Joule.

 

This approach unlocks a completely new way to ops  automation  where AI agents can reason, execute, and orchestrate operational tasks seamlessly following customers requests and needs.

The demonstration walks through a scenario about an S/4HANA Health Monitoring Ops Agent in Joule, designed to:

  • Provide system health & diagnostics insights;
  • Perform automated analysis & deliver actionable recommendations;
  • Proactively send alerts when potential issues are detected;

📖Just the beginning

 There is an unlimited range of Ops Agent scenarios enabled through SAP Automation Pilot, including for example:

  • SAP BTP Operations (e.g. Cloud Foundry applications,  SAP HANA Cloud, SAP cTMS, etc.);
  • SAP Build and Day 2 Operations for your apps;
  • SAP Integration Suite checks and actions via automated API calls; 
  • Integrations with custom solutions;
  • And many more …

Stay tuned as we will be revealing more real-life agentic operations scenarios in the coming weeks.

🚀 Get involved: Join Our Customer Engagement Initiative

If you would like to bring your own automation scenario and experience first-hand the AI-driven operations capabilities of SAP Automation Pilot and Joule, join our Customer Engagement Initiative: Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot

⏳Registration is open until March 15, 2026 - secure your spot soon!

 

Learn More

If you would like to explore MCP servers in more detail or start using them in your own landscapes, please check out the SAP Automation Pilot official documentation which provides further insights:

  • MCP Server Overview;
  • Managing MCP Servers;
  • Integrating SAP Automation Pilot with Joule Studio ;

Looking forward to collaborating and shaping the future of agentic IT operations together.

entry.title: 🤖⚡ Meet Traceability Agent, my first custom Joule Agent for SAP Digital Manufacturing. ⚡🤖
entry.content:
Introduction

This is not a dashboard.
This is not a report.
This is an AI Agent that understands your manufacturing history instantly.

Traceability Agent is an expert in as-built and batch traceability, capable of answering complex questions in seconds:

🔎Top-down: What went into this SFC, batch, or assembly?
🔁Bottom-up: Where was this batch, material, or component used?
⚡Instant analysis across orders, SFCs, batches, and inventory
🧠 No manual investigation. No digging. Just ask.

What used to take minutes or hours… now takes seconds.
This is a massive leap toward AI-driven manufacturing intelligence, where Joule Agents become true digital experts embedded directly into SAP Digital Manufacturing.

What are Joule Agents?

Joule Agents are specialized, generative AI-powered, and collaborative agents from SAP designed to automate complex, non-deterministic business workflows across finance, supply chain, procurement, and HR. They function as intelligent, context-aware teammates that use SAP Knowledge Graph and business data to proactively resolve multi-step tasks, interact with systems, and make decisions.

Learn more: Joule Agents: How SAP Uniquely Delivers AI Agents That Truly Mean Business
Demo: Joule Agents: Dispute Resolution Agent

Key aspects of Joule agents include:

  • Context-Aware Automation: Unlike generic AI, Joule agents are deeply integrated with SAP data, understanding how actions in one area, such as the supply chain, affect others, like finance.
  • Proactive Action: These agents can operate independently in the background, identifying exceptions, sending alerts, and preparing recommendations without constant user prompting.
  • Collaboration: Joule agents can work together, with some agents calling upon others to solve, for example, a complex, multi-functional project that requires both procurement and logistics expertise.
  • Customization: Using Joule Studio's agent builder, companies can build, test, and deploy their own custom agents to meet specific needs if pre-built options are unavailable.
  • Use Cases: Examples include the ExpenseIt Agent for analyzing receipts, Career and Talent Development Agents for HR planning, and Meeting Location Planner Agents in SAP Concur.

Joule agents are accessed through conversational interfaces, acting as an extension of the broader Joule AI assistant.

Learn more about Joule Studio, Agent Builder, and take Joule Studio Product Tour.

How I built Traceability Agent?

Joule Agents can easily leverage Joule Skills out-of-the-box as building blocks (Tools) to retrieve data and perform actions. However, for Traceability Agent, I wanted to try something new. For the first time, I wanted to explore MCP Servers and how that could enhance or improve not only my overall build flow, but also the overall outcome of the agent.

So, I ended up with the following architecture:

Where I deployed a MCP Server in SAP BTP:

That connects to SAP Digital Manufacturing MDOs (Manufacturing Data Objects), via HANA Database directly:

Which gives the access my Joule Agent would need to retrieve SAP Digital Manufacturing Analytics data.

With that in place, I was able to start building agents using Joule Studio.

First, defining Name, Description, Expertise and Instructions:

Then, defining my preferred LLM Model Settings, which in my case I used Google Gemini 2.5 Pro:

Then, adding the MCP Server mapping:

And adjusting my output preferences:

So I could then Test it, Release it and Deploy it.

So what!? What this Agent is capable of?

Glad you asked! 🙂 This agent can do really cool as-built and traceability analysis, including:

🔎Top-down: What went into this SFC, batch, or assembly?

🔁Bottom-up: Where was this batch, material, or component used?

🔝Top and Ranking Batches

and much more!

Now, I´m working on Nonconformance, Rework, OEE and Labor Agents, so stay tuned.

Conclusion

Traceability Agent shows how manufacturing intelligence is evolving from manual investigation to instant, conversational insight.

What once required navigating multiple screens and reports can now be answered in seconds by an AI Agent that understands your SAP Digital Manufacturing data and context. By combining Joule Agents, Joule Studio, and MCP Servers, this approach enables AI-powered experts that can analyze traceability, identify relationships, and deliver answers on demand.

 

What do you think about custom Joule Agents for SAP Digital Manufacturing? Let me know in the comments!

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Joule of the century - Supercharging SAP Analytics Cloud with artificial intelligence
entry.content:
What if your analytics solution didn’t just report the numbers… but worked alongside you?

In our upcoming session, “Joule of the century – Supercharging SAP Analytics Cloud with artificial intelligence”, we’ll explore how SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, and Joule are reshaping planning and analytics with embedded AI.

The thing is, we’re all navigating the same challenge: dashboards everywhere, insights nowhere.  AI changes that.  Generate KPIs in seconds.  Turn plain language into analysis.  Create formulas and SQL without the blank-page anxiety.  Even deploy autonomous AI agents that reason, plan, and act.

And yes, we’ll show how Joule moves from “Tell me how” to “Do this task” to “Give me insights” seamlessly inside your workflows.

If you’re working in xP&A and wondering how to bring real AI value to customers, this is the session.

Register now and see what happens when analytics finally feels… effortless.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013148.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPAnalyticsCloud #SAPDatasphere #BusinessAI #xPA

entry.title: What’s New in the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 release
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 release brings a new level of intelligence to everyday business work by expanding how AI supports users across various LoBs. At the core of this update is Joule and SAP’s agentic AI framework, where specialized agents and conversational capabilities work together to guide users through tasks, surface the right information, and provide recommendations in the moment of need. Instead of navigating complex transactions or piecing together data from multiple systems, employees can rely on natural‑language interactions to move through their processes faster and with greater confidence. This release also delivers embedded AI features that automate checks, streamline validations, and support operational decisions with real‑time insights. The overview chart below brings together all key innovations delivered with the SAP Cloud ERP Private and SAP Cloud ERP 2025 FPS01 Release, highlighting how AI, Joule capabilities, and embedded intelligence are supporting users across every line of business.

Altogether, these innovations help organizations reduce manual effort, strengthen transparency, and keep their operations running more smoothly, while allowing users to focus on the work that truly drives business outcomes.

Agentic use cases

Finance:

Mastering cash flow with the Cash Management Agent*

The Cash Management Agent supports finance teams by automating tasks that usually drain time, checking bank statement completeness, validating opening balances, and preparing cash‑flow forecasts. Many organizations struggle with fragmented financial data and delayed insights, which makes it difficult to spot potential cash shortages or opportunities to use surpluses effectively. By relying on Joule and Joule Agents to run these daily checks, calculations, and summaries, teams can shift from manual reconciliation to confident, data‑driven decisions. This automation improves accuracy and frees cash managers to focus on strategic liquidity planning instead of routine monitoring. In the end, it strengthens daily cash‑position visibility and helps organizations manage their cash resources more efficiently.

Learn more by accessing the SAP Help Portal.

* This AI offering is currently in Beta and available at no cost. Pricing will be communicated once the AI offering becomes generally available. A separate subscription is required for use or testing. Learn more.

 

R&D:

Streamlined engineering changes with the change record management*

Engineering teams often lose momentum when a simple product or process change turns into a maze of documentation, reviews, and approvals. Coordinators and engineers must manually create change records and shepherd them through the process, which can delay decisions and slow time‑to‑market. The Change Record Management Agent uses SAP Joule to assist with creating complete and consistent change records and orchestrating the necessary workflow steps. By reducing manual effort and guiding teams through the process, it helps engineering organizations keep changes moving without administrative bottlenecks. This leads to a faster, more efficient change‑management cycle and ultimately supports quicker delivery of updates and innovations.


 Use our SAP Help page to learn more.

 

Joule capabilities

Please note that certain Joule features described in this blog may not yet be available in your system and are planned for release as part of the upcoming Joule release cycle scheduled for March/April 2026.

Finance:

Joule for invoicing specialists to enable more-seamless navigation and transactional actions within billing documents

Invoicing specialists often spend far too much time clicking through multiple screens just to find the right convergent invoicing app or complete a billing‑related task. When workloads are high, even simple actions, like navigating to a billing document or making a small update, can slow the process and impact how quickly customers receive clear and accurate invoices. With expanded Joule capabilities, specialists can move directly to the right SAP Fiori apps and perform transactional actions through natural‑language prompts, without hunting for the correct tile or transaction. This conversational support reduces manual effort and helps teams resolve billing tasks more efficiently, leading to quicker processing and a better customer experience. Over time, this can even help reduce days sales outstanding by keeping billing workflows running smoothly and consistently.

 

AI-assisted accrual object creation

Preparing manual accruals often requires finance teams to gather fragmented information, apply judgment, and cross‑check historical patterns, all of which can be time‑consuming and inconsistent across entities. When deadlines are tight, variations in how preparers estimate amounts or select accounts can increase the risk of missed accruals or adjustments later in the audit process. With AI‑assisted accrual creation in Joule, users can describe their scenario in natural language and instantly receive a proposed accrual object, including category, amount, life term, and relevant GL accounts. They can refine any attribute, and Joule adapts its suggestions in real time based on the edits, gradually improving proposal quality through continued use. This guided, conversational approach streamlines preparation, increases consistency, and helps finance teams create more accurate accruals with far less effort.

 

 

Sales:

Staying ahead with AI-assisted detecting of expiring prices using Joule 

Pricing specialists often spend valuable time hunting through condition records to find prices that are about to expire, a task that becomes even more demanding when large volumes of data and tight sales timelines are involved. Missed expirations can lead to outdated pricing in customer quotes, last‑minute firefighting, and unnecessary manual corrections. With Joule detecting expiring prices, displaying relevant condition records, and even supporting validity extensions and mass changes, specialists can handle follow‑up activities with far less effort. This reduces repetitive work, minimizes human error, and keeps pricing information accurate and up to date across the system. Ultimately, it enables teams to manage pricing proactively, saving both time and operational cost.

 

 

Use our SAP Help page to learn more.

 

AI-assisted fetching and listing of open sales documents

Internal sales reps often need to scan through multiple criteria in VA05 just to find the right open sales orders, which can slow down workflows when customers are waiting for updates. Searching by document type, dates, materials, or organizational data requires navigating several fields and filters, making the process repetitive and time‑consuming. With AI‑assisted retrieval in Joule, reps can simply ask for open sales documents, and the system instantly displays orders filtered by attributes such as customer, material, creation date, or sales area. This conversational approach reduces the manual effort needed to locate the right information and helps reps respond faster and more accurately. The result is a more efficient sales process and smoother daily operations for the team.

 Consult our SAP Help Page for more information.

AI-assisted sales quotation changes using Joule 

Updating sales quotations can be surprisingly time‑consuming, especially when internal sales reps need to adjust quantities, refine pricing, change delivery dates, or capture customer‑specific references across multiple line items. These routine updates often involve navigating several fields in VA22, increasing the chance of small mistakes that can slow down the sales cycle. With AI‑assisted quotation changes in Joule, reps can make these adjustments through simple natural‑language requests, whether they need to update a quantity, modify a price via condition type, set delivery dates, or record reasons for rejection. This conversational flow speeds up the process and reduces manual effort, helping teams respond to customer requests more quickly and accurately. The result is a smoother quotation‑management experience that keeps sales moving with less friction.

 

Service:

Searching and navigating to service contracts with Joule

Service agents often spend more time searching for the right service contract than actually solving the customer’s issue, especially when navigating by status, dates, or complex contract structures. These delays can disrupt workflows and make it harder to move quickly from one task to the next. With Joule assisting through natural‑language prompts, agents can instantly search for service contracts, open details, complete releases, or jump directly to the relevant SAP Fiori functionality. This reduces unnecessary clicks and helps users focus on completing service‑related tasks instead of trying to find them. As a result, teams can deliver faster responses and manage service contracts with more confidence and less effort.

 

 

Joule for transferring in-house service objects

Transferring an in‑house service object between plants or storage locations often requires navigating several steps in the system, from creating the outbound delivery to recording the inbound receipt. These tasks can slow down technicians and coordinators who simply want to keep service operations moving. With Joule guiding the process through conversational prompts, users can initiate the transfer, add the target plant and storage location, and create the necessary deliveries without searching for the right transaction. This reduces friction in daily logistics and helps teams complete handovers more reliably. The conversational flow not only speeds up execution but also makes the transfer process easier for occasional users who don’t perform it every day.

 Consult our SAP Help Page for more information.

 

Supply Chain:

Enabling Joule to drive operational efficiency in outbound processes in SAP Extended Warehouse Management

Warehouse teams often juggle multiple screens just to track outbound deliveries, check warehouse tasks, or confirm the status of orders—slowing down operations in environments where every minute counts. As outbound volumes grow and customer expectations tighten, the effort required to manually search, summarize, and update records can become a real bottleneck. With Joule integrated into SAP EWM, users can search for outbound delivery orders, review warehouse tasks, or even create and confirm warehouse orders simply by asking in natural language. This gives frontline teams instant insights and reduces the time spent navigating complex menus or transactions. The result is faster decision‑making, smoother outbound processing, and a more intuitive experience that helps warehouses keep goods moving efficiently.

 To learn more, access our SAP Help Portal.

 

R&D:

Project assistant capabilities for the Joule copilot: read cost and revenue details of project objects

Project controllers and R&D teams often spend a lot of time gathering cost and revenue details from multiple reports, especially when they need to compare actuals, planned values, commitments, or fiscal‑year breakdowns. Navigating through different project objects, whether a project, WBS element, network, or network activity, can slow decision‑making and make routine checks feel heavier than they need to be. With Joule acting as a project assistant, users can simply ask for the financial picture of any project object and instantly receive details such as actual cost, planned cost, commitments, or revenue, including breakdowns by fiscal year, cost component, or value category. This conversational access removes the need to open complex reports or consolidate information manually, giving teams a clearer view of project performance in seconds. The result is faster insights, easier financial monitoring, and a more intuitive way for project stakeholders to stay on top of their work.

 Consult our SAP Help Page for more information.

 

Streamlining batch master insights and navigation with Joule

Reviewing batch master data often requires users to navigate across multiple fields and apps to check details such as material, plant, batch status, manufacturing date, shelf-life expiry date, or supplier, which can slow decision-making and increase the risk of missed insights under tight timelines. With Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, users can describe their requirement in natural language and instantly retrieve relevant batch master information, then navigate directly to the Manage Batches object page for deeper analysis, updates, or next actions. This conversational approach streamlines access to batch insights, enhances user experience, and improves operational efficiency through faster, more intuitive interaction.

 

Embedded AI

Finance:

AI-assisted fixed asset key figures explanation

Fixed asset reporting often raises questions that require deep accounting knowledge, especially when values are influenced by midyear acquisitions, depreciation keys, or complex calculation rules. In many organizations, answering these questions means digging through configuration tables or asking specialists to explain how a specific number was derived. With AI‑assisted explanations in Joule, users can simply request a breakdown of any fixed asset key figure and immediately see where the values come from and how they were calculated. This natural‑language guidance helps preparers and reviewers understand depreciation logic and valuation outcomes without manual analysis, reducing the risk of errors or compliance issues. As a result, teams can resolve fixed‑asset questions more quickly and consistently, with far less effort during period close or audits.

 

 

AI-assisted settlement rule proposal for asset capitalization  

Creating settlement rules for investment measures can be a painstaking task, especially when projects or internal orders need to settle to fixed assets and no automatic guidance is available. Finance teams often spend time determining the right receivers and percentages, increasing the risk of inconsistencies across measures or late adjustments when assets are capitalized. With AI‑assisted settlement rule proposals in Joule, users receive an initial suggestion for full settlement to fixed assets and can easily refine the proposal by adjusting percentages or selecting different receivers. This conversational support reduces manual effort and helps ensure more accurate asset class determination for final capitalization. The result is a smoother, more reliable settlement process that supports finance teams without adding complexity.

 

 

Supply Chain:

AI-assisted goods receipt analysis for bordero documents 

Goods receipt processing for bordero documents can be slow and error‑prone, especially when teams need to manually review carrier paperwork, create corresponding freight orders, and verify that all information matches what’s in the system. These manual checks often lead to delays at the gate, longer truck turnaround times, and unnecessary waiting for logistics partners. With SAP Document AI analyzing bordero documents, the system can automatically extract key details, propose a freight order, and highlight discrepancies between the uploaded document and existing freight data. This reduces the time spent on validations, improves accuracy, and helps teams process inbound goods more efficiently. The smoother flow not only enhances operational performance but can also reduce yard congestion by keeping vehicles moving.

 

Summary

As you look across the innovations in this release, a clear pattern emerges: AI is no longer something users “go to”, it’s something that quietly supports them throughout their day. Whether it’s a cash manager starting the morning with a clear liquidity picture, a service agent navigating directly to the right contract, or a warehouse coordinator checking outbound tasks with a simple question, Joule and embedded AI make these moments feel more connected and intuitive. These capabilities reduce the friction of everyday work, giving teams more room to focus on decisions, collaboration, and the tasks that genuinely move the business forward. And while this release delivers meaningful improvements on its own, it also lays the foundation for even more intelligent assistance, with agents and AI features becoming part of the natural rhythm of how people use SAP Cloud ERP. It’s a step toward a future where systems feel less like tools and more like partners, helping organizations work smarter, not harder. 

Thank you for taking the time to explore the latest updates of AI‑driven capabilities in SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition with us. If you’d like to stay informed about upcoming features, deep dives, and enablement content, make sure to follow updates tagged with PSCC_Enablement in the SAP Community. You’re also welcome to connect with me (@Yannick_PTT) both in the community and on LinkedIn for more insights and continuous updates.

For a deeper look into the intelligent capabilities delivered in this release, you can explore the full documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

More information

entry.title: How to train SAP Joule to act as your weatherman
entry.content:
Friday afternoon fun with SAP Joule, can I make Joule share weather conditions? In order to do so I need three things:

  1. SAP BTP with Joule activated
  2. A weather API
  3. A new Joule skill

In the video below I share a step by step approach to get your weather conditions in SAP Joule.

  • An SAP BTP destination to the weather API
  • An SAP Build Action to call the API
  • An SAP Joule Skill

Next is to get a proposal on what to wear based on weather conditions 😀

entry.title: Automating SAP HANA Cloud (Other Environment) DB Lifecycle Management with SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content:
In our recent updates around AI-enabled Ops Operations, we demonstrated how SAP Automation Pilot boosts structured, scalable, and intelligent automation for Day-2 Ops Agents through Joule.

Today, I am excited to take this one step further.

🚀  We are expanding our automation coverage to fully address SAP HANA Cloud lifecycle management in the Other Environment - bringing operational excellence to database management at scale.

From Standard Catalogs to Broader Lifecycle Automation

SAP Automation Pilot already provides a built-in catalog for managing SAP HANA Cloud database instances running in the Cloud Foundry environment - that is the the well-known SAP BTP Database Lifecycle Management (dblm-sapcp) Catalog.

This catalog has successfully helped customers automate essential lifecycle tasks directly within SAP BTP.

However, many customers also operate SAP HANA Cloud instances in the Other Environment, where lifecycle management is equally critical and operationally sensitive. While SAP Automation Pilot already offers all the technical capabilities required to automate those tasks, we recognized the need to package common scenarios into a structured, reusable, and publicly available catalog.

That is exactly what we have now delivered! 

Introducing: SAP HANA Cloud Lifecycle Management Sample Catalog (Other Environment)

We have consolidated a wide range of SAP HANA Cloud lifecycle scenarios into a dedicated public sample catalog, now available in our GitHub repository here: SAP HANA Cloud Lifecycle Management Sample Catalog (Other Environment)

This catalog provides a comprehensive and validated set of automation commands for managing SAP HANA Cloud instances at subaccount level using Service Manager.

Instead of executing repetitive and sensitive database operations manually, you can now automate domains such as: 🔹 Instance Lifecycle Management 🔹Backup & Recovery 🔹 Snapshot Management 🔹 Instance Configuration🔹 Upgrades & Plugins 🔹 Elastic Compute Nodes (ECN), etc. 

✅ All commands are validated and ready to be explored by our users - to help you minimize manual effort, reduce risk, and standardize operational procedures.

Beyond Automation: Toward Intelligent Database Operations

While structured lifecycle automation already delivers immediate value, the real transformation begins when automation becomes agentic.

Because all HANA Cloud-related commands are exposed through SAP Automation Pilot, they can be orchestrated, combined, and consumed in higher-level operational workflows. 

This naturally leads to the next evolution - AI Ops Agents for HANA Cloud enabled with SAP Automation Pilot and Joule! 

📖Be Part of the Journey - Join Our Customer Engagement Initiative

As we continue expanding agentic AI operations, collaboration with customers remains key.

If you would like to gain hands-on experience with AI-enabled Ops automation and help shape the future of intelligent database operations, we invite you to subscribe in our ongoing Customer Engagement Initiative: Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot

Through this initiative, you can:

  • Explore real-world AI-driven automation scenarios;
  • Bring your own use cases and operational challenges;
  • Provide direct feedback to the product team;
  • Influence upcoming capabilities;

⏳ Registration is open until March 15, 2026 - secure your spot soon!

From structured lifecycle automation to fully agentic database operations - this is the next chapter of operational excellence with SAP Automation Pilot.

Let’s build it together!

entry.title: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers
entry.content:
The Basic Trial offering for SAP Build has been extended with a new workbook called 'Fusion Development with ABAP Cloud in SAP Build'. You can explore this workbook for the Basic Trial offering around SAP Build, that shows how developers can now create new ABAP Cloud projects, or work on existing ones, from the SAP Build lobby and then navigate to their ABAP development environment – the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (ADT).

Task 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account and add the ABAP instance in ADT.

Click the following URL:   

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/build/trial.html

In the following screen click on "Try Now". 

You will receive a user with a password that will allow you to authenticate at the landing page of the basic trial in the respective region for your user, e.g. https://trials.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/ .  

Basic Trial Registration

By using the credentials presented in the web page you will get access to some workbooks that cover different scenarios. 

The ABAP related workbook is named Fusion Development with ABAP Cloud in SAP Build.

Select the highlighted workbook.

Choose the Highlighted Workbook

Click ‘Start Workbook’

Start WorkBook

Note : It may take up to 30 minutes to fully activate your user account in the SAP BTP ABAP Environment. 

Read the details mentioned in Overview of the Section ‘Set up your development environment’. You can skip Step 1 and 2 if you already have ADT installed but make sure you are on the latest version of ADT for all the features to work fine. Once the installation is done directly move to Step 3 - 'Create ABAP Cloud Project in SAP Build' only until adding the basic trial instance to ADT. We will be it using the URL marked in the below screenshot.

Note:- Once you have the system added in ADT you can close the workbook.

System URL

Task 2 :  Create a package in the new ABAP Cloud Project 
Once you see the new ABAP Cloud Project (TDI ) added in ADT, create a package with the naming convention ZAC_<your community ID>.

Note: Please make sure you are using the latest version of ADT, otherwise package creation will not work.

To get the community ID launch the URL : https://community.sap.com/ and click on the person icon as shown in below screenshot and go to ‘My Content’.

Community Page

Once the below page opens up please copy the number after user-id in the URL to get your community ID.

Community ID

Note:- We will be validating your activities by checking the folder against your community ID. So make sure all your development objects are saved inside this package.

Task 3 :  Learn about the SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities

To prepare for the challenge familiarise yourself with the scope first by going through the following help document.

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/joule-for-developers-abap-ai-capabilities-f14ebffef77b41bfb0746c33dcb70e84?locale=en-US

Now you are ready to take up the challenge. See you next week !!

entry.title: ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial
entry.content:
Let’s kick off this year’s Developer Challenge with a topic close to home: ABAP.

With AI dominating conversations across the tech world, we thought it would be exciting for our community to explore Joule for Developers this month.

SAP Joule for Developers brings embedded AI capabilities to SAP Build and ABAP, delivered through Joule. It helps accelerate the development of apps and extensions, reduce development costs, and enhance overall developer productivity and proficiency.

SAP Joule for Developers is engineered for ABAP excellence by

  • Leveraging SAP’s ABAP large language model trained on enterprise-grade code.
  • Generating data models, app logic, and unit tests with precision.
  • Migrating custom ABAP code faster with documentation chat, code explanation, and issue diagnostics.

The Challenge:

  For this challenge we will be using Basic Trial offering for SAP Build that includes a SAP BTP ABAP Environment with Joule for Developers. During the first week we will focus on setting up the ABAP instance from the Basic Trial in ADT and then will explore some of the key functionalities of Joule for Developers.

How to Participate:

To participate in and complete this challenge, simply follow these steps:

  • Complete the challenge assigned for each week.
  • Create a package with your community ID in the format mentioned in the Week1 challenge.
  • Post your package name in the thread for each week.
  • We will review your work inside the package with your community ID.

Challenge Timeline

The challenge will span for 4 weeks starting on Monday March 2nd 2026 and ending on March 23rd 2026. We will keep the challenge open till the end of the month to accomodate anyone who is running late 🙂. To count towards the overall monthly challenge, you must complete and submit each week's task. Make sure to revisit this blog post to discover the upcoming week's challenge!

March 2nd, 2026   – Start of Challenge: Week 1

March 9th, 2026    –  Week 2 

March 16th, 2026  – Week 3 :

March 23rd, 2026  – Week 4 :

So let’s get started.

Rewards

Upon submitting all required entries by the end of the challenge, you'll earn an SAP Community badge for participating in the SAP Developer Challenge for March. Please ensure to follow all the instructions mentioned in this blogpost or the respective challenge discussions.

We are excited to hear your experience about the challenge and the learning adventure.

Best of Luck !!!

entry.title: From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This February
entry.content:
In the age of AI, the skills we build today will define how we work tomorrow. That’s why we’ve launched a monthly blog series that spotlights the latest AI learning assets and upcoming learning opportunities.

Building on my first blog, where I introduced you to our AI live sessions Beyond Hype (which is all about getting productive with SAP Business AI), I wanted to address some of your most asked questions:

  1. What is the difference between Joule skills and Joule agents?

    Joule skills are designed for executing single, predefined tasks independently. These skills enhance SAP's default capabilities by leveraging unstructured inputs to perform specific, context-aware actions, such as data retrieval or system transactions, in a deterministic, predictable manner. Joule Skills integrates with both SAP and third-party APIs to automate and streamline operations such as ticket creation and workflow execution, all initiated through conversational interactions.

    Joule Agents are AI applications that assist business users with decision support and task automation. They use LLMs and reasoning models to determine actions and their sequence, addressing various situations autonomously. Joule Agents can autonomously execute complex multi-step business processes. They engage users to review and approve significant actions that impact the business, such as extending discounts to dissatisfied customers. User requests or specific business events, such as the creation of a new sales order or customer service ticket, can trigger Joule Agents.

    Deep dive by watching this video: What are Joule agents and skills?  

  2. How can we start from scratch with Joule Studio to create our own portfolio of Joule skills and Joule agents?

    To use Joule Studio, the SAP Build developer license (SKU 8019311) is required, and you need to install the SAP Build Process Automation service to leverage the Cloud Integration Automation service scenario for SAP Build. Please note that you need to be on the new unified SAP Build commercial model.

    And of course, you need Joule Base or Premium. Please note, Joule Studio works with any Joule brownfield license, except Joule for Consultants and Joule for Developers. To run custom Joule Skills in your production environments, you do not need AI Units. However, execution of custom Joule Agents will consume AI Units.

    To all Partners, a TDD license is available on the SAP Partner Portal! Read on to learn how to set up Joule Studio for all partner flows and entitlement levels. I am excited to share the link to the free 30-day basic trial. Request now and select workbook: Create a Joule Agent with Joule Studio!

    And lastly, here are the instructions to set up Joule Studio for all customer flows and entitlement levels.

  3. Besides building low-code and no-code agents with Joule Studio, can I also code them in Python?

    Of course, you can! Have a look at the SAP Community Blog Post by @noravonthenen  from the Developer Advocates. In her article, she shares her experience building an AI Agent with LiteLLM, CrewAI, and SAP-RPT-1, and what actually worked. You can also check out this GitHub repository and learn how to Build Your Own AI Agent-Based Solution using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI and the Generative AI Hub in AI Foundation.

However, let’s continue to focus on Joule Studio. In this three-part tutorial series, we will walk you through the complete journey of building your own custom AI agent in Joule Studio, from defining the business use case to extending its capabilities and finally bringing it to life.

Each part builds on the previous one. By the end, you won’t just understand the theory, you’ll have created a fully functional agent tailored to your scenario.

  1.  Define the use case: We start by grounding everything in a concrete scenario for Intelligent Maintenance Order Fulfillment. What problem are we solving? What outcome are we aiming for? This foundation ensures the agent is built with purpose.

     

  2. Extend the Joule Skill: Next, we move from concept to capability. You’ll learn how to extend an existing skill to support the custom requirements of your use case.

     

  3. Create the Joule Agent: Finally, we assemble everything into a working agent — connecting the extended skill and configuring the behavior so it can operate autonomously.

     

Want to dive deeper into the content? Rewatch the session and access the session material via SAP Learning Hub (subscription required) and explore your key resources to help you get started building your own Joule agents:

  • Step-by-step guidance to build custom Joule agents in Joule Studio (recording)
  • Joule Studio Goes Live: Answers to Your Top Questions covering Skills and the new Agent Builder features 
  • Best Practices for Building Agents: Key principles and practices for designing effective Joule Agents 
  • Discovery Center Mission: Get Hands-on experience in creating a Joule Agent with multiple skills and MCP
  • Since you also asked about a certification for Joule Studio, the SAP Build Developer Certification includes Joule Studio.

Hi Joule, where should I get started with the latest learning resources? *

Apart from live enablement sessions, get hands-on with our latest on-demand learning resources:

  • Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core, and Generative AI Hub.
  • Explore how to make Large Language Models truly enterprise-ready by grounding them with your own business data using Document Grounding in SAP Generative AI Hub.
  • Create your own schemas from scratch in the SAP Document AI workspace. Go ahead and try it yourself! 
  • Learn how to deploy an LLM on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad, the orchestration service, and the Python SDK.
  • Get started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python learn how the Machine Learning process develops using a Python Client for SAP HANA.
  • Get started with AI (Vector Engine, NLP, Knowledge Graph[soon]) using SAP HANA and Python, and learn how SAP HANA Cloud supports the development of generative AI applications.
  • Implement enterprise-grade AI solutions using SAP's AI services and integration with generative AI models.

 As you can see, some SAP Samples were used for an SAP CodeJam. An SAP CodeJam is a free, hands-on developer workshop organized by SAP that focuses on a specific technology topic. It’s designed for developers who want practical, guided experience — not just slides, but real coding. If you want to experience a SAP CodeJam in person, check out the upcoming events in your region and sign up!

* Powered by the machine learning technology of SAP AI Business Services, personalized recommendations are generated for employees, in one place on the Learning home page, based on employee data.

As we look ahead, we’re excited to share with you a new lineup of learning opportunities designed to help you expand your knowledge and make AI an advantage in your role:

  • March 5, 11 am CET: Accelerate your transformation to the Cloud with SAP Business AI. Discover how SAP’s AI-powered migration toolchain enables customers to accelerate their transformation with less risk and cost to the SAP Business Suite.
  • March 12, 11 am CET: Efficient Document Inbound Processing in SAP Cloud ERP with SAP Document AI. Explore the pre-delivered document inbound processing use cases for SAP Cloud ERP across Sales, Finance, and Quality Management.
  • March 19, 10 am CET: All Things Business AI: Latest Insights, Updates & Live Demos. Discover current AI trends, recent updates across SAP’s AI portfolio, and see live demos showcasing how Business AI can drive efficiency and innovation in real-world business processes.

Stay tuned for more, and happy learning! 

Further Resources:

  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This October
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This November
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This December
  • From Hype to Hands-On: Level Up Your AI Skills This January

Joule Studio SAP Business AI Joule  SAP Document AI AI Foundation #sapclouderp #handson

entry.title: Enterprise AI in Action: SAP Joule Delivers Where Others Don’t
entry.content:
AI is becoming part of everyday enterprise work, from drafting emails to designing complex processes. While ChatGPT and Gemini can generate functional specs and ABAP code, the real question is: “Why use SAP Joule?”
The difference is context – Joule is embedded in S/4HANA, understanding system configuration, business rules, workflows, and live data to deliver outputs that are accurate, compliant, and ready for real-world use. Unlike general-purpose AI, it doesn’t just produce well-written text; it grasps the technical and business landscape to ensure every recommendation aligns with your enterprise environment.
To understand this difference in practical terms, let’s look at two common S/4HANA scenarios: a Purchase Order compliance check and a Sales Order credit validation. On the surface, both appear straightforward – define the requirement, identify the enhancement spot, document the logic, and generate the necessary ABAP code.
A general-purpose AI tool can certainly help structure the functional specification and outline the technical approach. However, the real challenge in enterprise projects is not drafting the document – it is ensuring that the design aligns with the system’s actual configuration, enhancement framework, workflow setup, authorization model, and business data. This is where the distinction between generic AI and embedded, SAP-native intelligence becomes clear.

1. Purchase Order Compliance Check
Business Requirement: Block Purchase Order creation if:
• Vendor is not approved in a custom Z-table
• PO value exceeds 1,000,000
• Purchasing group = “A01”
• Mandatory attachment is missing
Using Chatgpt or Gemini:
• Enhancement via BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST
• Logic to check vendor approval, PO value, purchasing group, and attachment
• Error messages and test cases
Pros: Clear FS structure, general logic explained
Cons: Generic — it won’t know your actual Z-table structure, workflow configuration, or document type setup. You must feed these details manually.
Using SAP Joule:
• Identifies the correct BAdI or workflow in your S/4 system
• Pulls actual vendor master and custom table fields
• Checks your approval strategy and document type configuration
• Generates FS logic aligned with your live system setup
Pros: System-grounded, accurate, reduces rework, supports compliance

2. Sales Order Credit Validation
Business Requirement: Prevent Sales Order creation if a customer’s credit limit is exceeded.
Using Chatgpt or Gemini:
• Identify a relevant BAdI or user exit
• Check credit exposure against the limit
• Trigger an error message if the limit is exceeded
• Suggest test cases for validation
Pros: Clean structure, well-written, and easy to understand
Cons: Generic — it doesn’t know your actual credit control area, company code setup, or the exact fields and BAdIs in your S/4HANA system. All system-specific details must be manually added.
Using SAP Joule:
• Detect the active BAdI or enhancement spot in your system
• Pull the correct credit control configuration and field names
• Align logic with company code settings and authorization roles
• Suggest tests based on your live system data
Pros: Context-aware, system-aligned, lower risk of errors

Key Differentiators

  1. Verified SAP Knowledge Base: Responses are grounded in SAP's official documentation and verified sources.
  2. SAP-Specific Methodology: Understands SAP implementation approaches, configuration requirements, and technical constraints.
  3. Industry-Specific Compliance: Aligned with SAP's consulting standards and best practices.
  4. SAP Ecosystem Integration: Deep understanding of how SAP modules, technologies, and processes interconnect.

Comparison Table

Capability

SAP Joule for Consultants

ChatGPT

Gemini

Notes

SAP Domain Expertise

Deep, verified SAP knowledge from official sources

General SAP knowledge from public training data

General SAP knowledge with real-time search

Joule is trained on SAP-verified documentation

Template Generation

SAP-specific templates and methodologies

Generic business templates

Generic business templates with Google integration

Templates follow SAP best practices and standards

Functional Specification Writing

SAP implementation-focused with proper technical structure

General functional spec approaches

Structured but generic approaches

Joule understands SAP-specific requirements and constraints

Industry Standards Compliance

SAP best practices and implementation standards

General industry standards

General standards with research capabilities

Aligned with SAP's consulting methodologies

Technical Documentation Structure

SAP-aligned formats (ABAP, configuration paths, transaction codes)

Generic technical documentation

Structured technical docs

Joule provide SAP-specific technical details and paths

Integration Knowledge

Deep SAP ecosystem and module integration expertise

Broad but surface-level SAP integration knowledge

General integration knowledge

Joule understand SAP module interdependencies and best practices

Business Process Understanding

SAP module-specific processes and workflows

General business process knowledge

General business knowledge with research

Joule trained on SAP business process documentation

For functional specification writing in SAP contexts, Joule is the superior choice because:

  • Accuracy: knowledge comes from verified SAP sources rather than general internet training data. 
  • Relevance: understands SAP-specific technical requirements, configuration paths, and implementation constraints. 
  • Structure: can provide proper SAP documentation formats, including transaction codes, customization paths, and technical specifications. 
  • Best Practices: aligned with SAP's consulting methodologies and implementation standards.

We’ve seen how generic AI can draft specs and code, but context matters. In your S/4HANA projects, where has AI helped — and where has it fallen short? Share your experiences, challenges, or tips below!

Source:

SAP Help Portal | SAP Online Help

Joule

Other Blogs:

From Conversational AI to Business AI: Why SAP Jou... - SAP Community

System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA - SAP Community

entry.title: Why Generative UI is Redefining the Future of Enterprise Software
entry.content:
The world of user interfaces is evolving rapidly, spurred by the rise of consumer AI and shifting expectations for business software. Employees want the same intuitive and conversational experiences they enjoy at home to be reflected in their workplace tools. While text-based interactions are flexible and user-friendly, they struggle with structured data. Graphical user interfaces (GUIs) excel in presenting structured information, but they are rigid and costly to customize.

Enter generative UI: the game-changing solution bridging text and GUI. Generative UI allows software interfaces to dynamically adapt to each user’s intent, context, and task. By leveraging SAP Joule and Joule Agents, these interfaces materialize on demand, grounded in real-time, accurate data, while enabling seamless execution of complex workflows.

Generative UI is the future of enterprise software. It’s not just about dashboards—it’s about interfaces that empower users to make decisions and drive action with precision and confidence.

Read the full blog to learn how generative UI is redefining business applications.

entry.title: Troubleshooting Ariba-Joule Integration errors
entry.content:
Introduction:🚀 Cracking the SAP Ariba–Joule Integration common errors:

Over the past few months, I’ve seen a common pattern across multiple customer implementations: brilliant landscapes struggling with Navigation Flow glitches, incomplete Transactional prompts, or inconsistent User Sync—all caused by a handful of tiny but critical configuration gaps across Ariba, IAS, IPS, and Build Work Zone.

To help accelerate troubleshooting and avoid post‑go‑live surprises, I’ve put together a practical breakdown of the most recurring issues and the exact fixes that have consistently stabilized the integration.

If you're enabling Joule for Ariba or supporting customers on their journey, this article will save you hours of guesswork. 🔧💡

Common issues with Navigation flow and resolution: 

Scenario 1:Navigational prompts intermittently return data

Sample prompts: Go my approvals, my task, create engagement request, created sourcing project, open supplier profile etc   

Cause: This could happen, if user has been assigned more than 150 groups in Ariba and those groups are being replicated to IAS and then to Build work zone.  To verify if the issue is due to roles replicated to BWZ,  login to BWZ and user will get error “HTTP ERROR 431”. 

Resolution: Replicate only those standard groups to BWZ which are required for Ariba-Joule integration. You can achieve this by adding a filter parameter ariba.applications.group.filter in the IPS source system (the source system configured to fetch users and groups from Ariba https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-integration-with-sap-ariba-setup-guide/ba-p/14240003 ). Using this filter ensures that only the required groups—and users assigned to those groups—are synchronized from Ariba to IAS and subsequently to BWZ.

Sample value: (displayName eq "Sourcing Agent" or displayName eq "Sourcing Approver")

Important Notes

  • Navigational flow works only with standard groups.
  • If customers assign only custom roles to users in Ariba, they must still replicate the required standard groups from Ariba → IAS → BWZ.
  • Custom groups are not supported, SAP is considering for future release 
  • This filtering process does not impact customer‑specific role assignments:
    • Customers may continue using their custom groups assignment in Ariba as usual
    • Since custom groups map back to standard roles, any Joule‑relevant standard roles tied to custom roles will still function properly through this filtered sync.
  • Recommendation:
    • Maintain a list of standard groups required for Joule navigation.
    • Additionally replicate customer‑specific custom groups only to ensure that all Ariba users sync correctly to CIS

Steps to maintain the parameter:

  • Login to CIS and navigate to identity provision
  • Source system à look for the source system which is created to fetch the user/groups from Ariba
  • Edit and maintain the parameter and run the IPS job as described previously
List of standard roles which are required for Ariba joule Navigational scenario:

Supplier Registration Manager

Supplier Manager

Supplier Qualification Manager

Supplier Risk ESG User

Purchasing Administrator

Supplier Risk Engagement Requestor

Financial Processing Analyst

Supplier Risk Evaluation Expert

AuditBuyer

Supplier Risk Provider Registration Expert

Purchasing Agent

Joule Supplier Profile User

Invoice Manager

Sourcing Project Administrator

Purchasing Manager

Sourcing Agent

Report Manager

Limited Event Administrator

Receiving Manager

Junior Sourcing Agent

Manual Order Processor

SM Questionnaire Manager

Tax Manager

Supplier Risk Engagement Governance Analyst

Purchasing User

Supplier Qualification Request Manager

Executive Approver

Supplier Risk User

Sourcing Approver

Supplier Risk Report Expert

Category Buyer

Supplier Risk Engagement Expert

Supplier Risk Engagement Analyst

Sourcing Manager

Operational Junior Sourcing Agent

Supplier Risk Manager

Supplier Risk Configuration Expert

Supplier Request Manager

SM Process Manager

Event Administrator

Scenario 2:  Error “The application cannot be opened”

Resolution: Create a destination under the sub-account which has been created for joule. Make sure destination name is “NavigationService” follow the below article for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/integrating-joule-with-sap/configure-destination-to-use-navigation-service?locale=en-US&q=+NavigationService

Steps:

  • Login to Joule Sub-account
  • Click connectivity à Destination
  • Click create

Scenario 3: Error: You need different permissions to perform this action or I am having trouble processing your request.

Cause : This happen when user doesn’t have the required access in Ariba or if they have the required access, joule related standard roles are not assigned to user in BWZ after replication.  

To verify the roles in BWZ, login to BWZ

  • Check User Role Assignments: In the settings à Identity Provisioning
  • Within "Explore Role Assignments",enter the user ID experiencing the issue
  • Review the list of roles and permissions assigned to the user

Resolution: As described in scenario 1 navigation flow, maintain the joule roles in source system parameter run the job. This will synchronize the user and groups between ariba , IAS and BWZ. Make sure roles are assigned to users in ariba as well. Login to BWZ and verify the roles once again:

Common issues with Transactional flow and resolution:

Scenario 1:When user enters any transactional prompt in joule, user gets results as an informational data.

Sample prompts: List of recent purchases

Cause: This happens when ibn_target not found at the backend. In simple words, the formation synchronization did not happen between the include system despite the fact status of the formation is ready.  

Resolution: Login to global account, exclude and include Ariba buyer and sourcing system within the formations.

 

Scenario 2: When user enters a transactional prompt, there is no response

Cause: This happens when there is no dependencies added to the das-ias applications in CIS

Resolution:

  • Login to IAS
  • Navigate to applications and search for “das-ias” and look for the entry which is there for the sub-account which is created for joule
  • Navigate to dependency section, make sure to maintain APIs for Procurement and Sourcing applications

Delayed User synchronization between Ariba and Cloud identify service: IPS Jobs are getting stuck in running status for few hours  

Steps to run the Job:

  • Login to CIS and navigate to identity provision

Source system à look for the source system which is created to fetch the user/groups from Ariba

 Click on job logs in IPS to check the status:

 Resolution:  By default the value configured for parameter (ariba.applications.group.members.page.size,ariba.applications.user.groups.page.size )  is 50, update it according to your need. If this doesn’t work, log a support ticket with SAP to increase the API rate limit.

Joule Icon Is Missing in Ariba despite completing all the configurations (running boosters, system added successfully in formations, SSO is enabled)

Resolution: Log a ticket with SAP to enable the feature PLUI 2928  at parent and child level

If you are facing any other issues, I would suggest to log a case with support team. To save time, make sure to share the logs and X-Correlation ID with support team. 

How to download the Joule Logs:

  • Login to Ariba application
  • Click on joule icon, reproduce the issue
  • Click on three bars --> settings  --> abouts --> download trances --> include message history --> download 

How to Get X Correlation ID?

  • Login to Ariba system
  • Enable logs:
    • Right-click on browser, select "Inspect"
    • once the Developer Tools are open, locate and click on the "Network" tab
  • Open Joule (keep the Developer Tools open)
  • Reproduce the issue you are experiencing
  • When the issue is reproduced:
    • in the Network tab, identify the most recent API call to Joule. This API call will be named with a UUID (for example: c1234b04-c313-42cd-9be3-a5efe9b0f2d1).
    • click on the API request and go to the "Headers" section;
    • scroll down to find and collect the following fields:
      • X-correlationid
      • Timestamp,
      • JouleDataCenter (you can find this in the "API Request URL")

Ariba-Joule support queues: you may choose the queue based on the nature of the error. 

✨Conclusion
The most common Ariba–Joule issues often stem from small but critical configuration gaps across Ariba, IAS, IPS, and Build Work Zone—and fixing these resolves the majority of navigation, transactional, and sync failures.
With the right configurations in place, Joule delivers the seamless, intelligent experience it’s designed for—helping customers unlock real value without post‑go‑live roadblocks. 🚀

entry.title: Introducing prebuilt Joule skills in SAP Build digital workspaces: Get more done with simple prompts
entry.content:
If your day involves spinning up new spaces, hunting for the right workspace, or sharing content with your team, you know the clicks can add up. SAP Build simplifies this in its digital workspace experience with new prebuilt Joule skills which turns plain‑language prompts into actions. Ask Joule to create or find a workspace and it does the work in the background- then gives you a direct link in the chat so you can jump right in

The first set of prebuilt Joule skills are already live. Take a look at what’s available today: 

 

What’s in this first set of skills

  • Create workspaces from a prompt
    • Example: “Create a workspace called ‘Q3 Onboarding Hub’ using the standard onboarding template.”
    • Joule creates and names the workspace behind the scenes and returns a link in the chat.
  • Find workspaces you need
    • Example: “Show me all workspaces I’m a member of related to ‘Onboarding’.”
    • Joule lists relevant workspaces right in the chat, helping you get where you need to go faster.
  • Add documents right from chat
    • Drag and drop a file into the Joule chat, confirm the target workspace, and Joule adds it for you- no tab hopping required.
  • Find and add members to workspaces
    • Tell Joule who you want to add to a workspace, and Joule finds the person, confirms and sends them an invitation to join the workspace.

Why this matters

For teams needing a space to collaborate and produce results, these skills simplify their day-to-day so that -

  • members spend less time on common setup tasks like launching a project hub, onboarding center, or campaign space,
  • users can move from “search and navigate” to “ask and act” without leaving their flow of work,
  • workspace creators get consistency in content through standardized workspace templates to get started quickly, 
  • business users get more self-service options and admins get fewer “how do I create/find X?” tickets.

Prebuilt skills can be used in conjunction with the existing AI capabilities already available in digital workspaces. For example, once you find or create workspaces via Joule, you can use embedded AI assistance to create and publish content or summarize documents within the workspace.

Further, Joule can be enabled with document grounding for workspaces so that it answers questions based on content maintained in workspaces and links back to sources. This way its responses are grounded and relevant to business context.

Admins and developers can also build custom skills for Joule using Joule Studio and use them in digital workspaces.

Everyday scenarios where you can use these skills:

  • HR onboarding hub: “Create a workspace called ‘New Hire Hub – July’ based on the onboarding template.” Share the link with HR/ team managers in minutes.
  • Project kickoff: “Find all workspaces mentioning ‘Apollo Launch’.” Jump straight into the right workspace for plans, assets, and status.
  • Policy refresh: “Create a workspace named ‘FY26 Policy Updates’.” Drag and drop updated PDFs into the chat to populate the workspace content folder quickly.
  • Campaign workspace: “Create ‘Q2 Partner Enablement’.” Then use AI writing assistance on the landing page to publish a clean, on‑brand overview.
  • Knowledge triage: “Show my ‘Customer Escalations’ workspaces.” Get a list in chat and dive into the relevant one to update content or add the right members to solve the issue.

Getting started with Joule’s prebuilt skills and embedded AI in digital workspaces:

    • First, the admin must ensure Work Zone is enabled as a Joule launcher as per the instructions in this guide
    • Once set up is done, open Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and try prompts like
      • “Create a workspace named ‘Team All‑Hands’.”
      • “Find my workspaces related to ‘Compliance’.”
    • Ask your admin to enable document grounding for the workspaces you rely on- so Joule can answer questions with citations.
    • Ask your admin to enable embedded AI features to use AI assistance when editing pages or posts to speed up content creation once your workspace is ready or to summarize your documents quickly. See how you can use embedded AI for document summarization or workspace content creation here: 

 

Tips for Best Results for interacting with Joule:

  • Be specific with search keywords while searching for workspaces or content with Joule
  • While prompting give sufficient context to help Joule understand the task.

What’s next
This is just the first set of prebuilt skills we’re releasing for digital workspaces. We’re continuing to expand the catalog with more actions and smarter multi-step flows.

entry.title: Two Ways to Build Agentic Reporting in SAP: Python Agent on SAP BTP vs. Joule Studio Agent
entry.content:
1. Introduction

In my previous blog post on Agentic Reporting, I talked about how modern reporting goes beyond dashboards — it requires systems that reason, explain, and act.

In this follow‑up, instead of going straight into a specific implementation, I want to answer a bigger question:

What are the different ways to build an AI Agent in the SAP ecosystem?

With SAP’s evolving AI landscape, you now have two powerful paths to build enterprise‑grade agents:

Option A — Build a Python Agent on SAP BTP

Custom, full‑code, flexible Agent service that integrates with SAP Datasphere via SQL or OData.

Option B — Build an Agent directly in SAP Joule Studio

A no‑code/low‑code agent builder inside SAP Build for reasoning, orchestration, and multi‑system actions.

Both options are valid. Both can support Agentic Reporting. But they differ significantly in architecture, flexibility, governance, and effort.

This post compares both, so you can choose the best path for your use case.

2. The Two Agent‑Building Paths Explained

Option A — Python Agent on SAP BTP (Full‑Code + Maximum Flexibility)

This is the classic approach where you build an application in Python, deploy it on SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry or Kyma), and connect it to SAP Datasphere.

When is this ideal?

  • Heavy data processing
  • Custom ML/AI logic
  • Integration with complex non-SAP systems
  • You want maximum control over logic, dependencies, libraries
  • Advanced reasoning models + custom frameworks
  • You want to host an API that other tools can call (including Joule)

What your Python Agent can do

  • Connect to SAP Datasphere using:
    • SQL (hdbcli)
    • OData Analytical or Relational APIs
  • Execute complex logic (anomaly detection, simulations, scoring)
  • Write data back to Open SQL schema
  • Expose REST endpoints for other SAP systems and Joule agents to consume

Architecture Summary

  • Python app on BTP
  • Service bindings → HANA Cloud credentials via VCAP_SERVICES
  • OAuth → Datasphere OData access
  • HANA Cloud SQL → read/write via Open SQL schema
  • Python performs reasoning, actions, decisions

Option B — Joule Studio Agent (SAP-native no‑code Agent Builder)

SAP Joule Studio allows business teams and architects to build agents without needing to write any code.

These agents can:

  • Plan
  • Reason
  • Execute workflows
  • Call APIs (via Joule Skills)
  • Use SAP Knowledge Graph
  • Use Document Grounding
  • Orchestrate SAP and non‑SAP tools

When is this ideal?

  • You want an SAP-native, governed agent
  • You want quick setup (no DevOps, no runtime)
  • You want conversational + actionable agents
  • You want orchestration across SAP applications
  • You want to avoid managing Python runtimes
  • You want automatic reasoning and traceability

What your Joule Agent can do

  • Connect to SAP systems via destinations
  • Invoke Joule Skills (API calls, BTP actions)
  • Consume Datasphere data via OData APIs
  • Perform reasoning using SAP’s LLMs
  • Automate workflows in SuccessFactors, S/4HANA, Ariba, etc.

Architecture Summary

  • Joule Studio = Agent Builder
  • Uses Destinations for integration
  • Uses Skills & Actions for API calls
  • LLM-powered agentic reasoning

No deploy, no runtime maintenance

3. Comparing the Two Approaches

4. Which Option Should You Use?

When to Choose Python Agent on BTP

Technical Complexity Requirements:

  • Advanced algorithmic logic - When you need custom algorithms beyond standard business logic
  • Heavy transformations/ETL - For complex data processing workflows requiring full programmatic control 
  • External ML libraries - Integration with sklearn, PyTorch, transformers, or other specialized libraries 

Integration Scenarios:

  • API endpoint creation - When other systems need to call your agent as a service
  • Any system connectivity - Leveraging the "any API, any system" capability for non-SAP integrations 

When to Choose Joule Studio Agent

Rapid Development Needs:

  • Fast deployment with the "lower effort, no-code" approach 
  • Built-in agentic reasoning eliminating custom development overhead 

Enterprise Governance:

  • SAP-native governance & logging for compliance and auditability 
  • Built-in trace, executions, reasoning view for operational transparency 
  • Skill marketplace access for pre-built capabilities 

SAP Ecosystem Integration:

  • SAP-first integrations with S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Ariba 
  • SAP Knowledge Graph + Documents for automatic grounding 
  • Workflow automation & orchestration as the primary use case

5. Conclusion:

SAP’s Agentic AI is evolving rapidly, and you now have two mature ways of building enterprise‑grade agents:

1. Python Agent on SAP BTP

Flexible, powerful, code-based, perfect for analytics and complex logic.

2️. Joule Studio Agent

SAP-native, governed, no-code, perfect for orchestration, reasoning, and functional automation.

Both are valid choices and can even work together.

 

Next in the series:

  • Blog #3: How to Build a Python Agent for Agentic Reporting (Datasphere + BTP)
  • Blog #4: How to Build an SAP-native Joule Agent for Agentic Reporting

Previous Blog in the series:

Blog #1: Agentic Reporting - The Next Evolution of SAP Reporting

entry.title: SAP 2026: Between standard power and individual competitive advantage
entry.content:
The world of SAP SE is changing. While SAP stock and the SAP dividend dominate the headlines of the financial press, companies are facing a much deeper question: How much standardization is necessary and how much individuality is permissible?

At Toraman Consulting, we follow a clear principle: The SAP standard is the foundation, but we don't condemn in-house developments . It's about maintaining a balance.

The foundation: Why the standard is more important today than ever

With the rise of SAP Cloud and innovations like Joule (SAP's new AI assistant), a clean core is becoming essential. Those who are too deeply entrenched in the system code of the old SAP R/3 world cannot fully leverage the advantages of S/4HANA and the new Joule agents . The standard ensures stability, update capability, and access to state-of-the-art functions in modules such as SAP MM , SAP SD , and SAP PP .

The optional part: Where in-house developments make the difference

But we also know that a company isn't successful simply by doing the exact same thing as everyone else. Real competitive advantages often lie in processes that are anything but run-of-the-mill.

Our philosophy at Toraman Consulting :

  • Weighing options instead of rejecting: We carefully examine where the standard is sufficient and where a targeted extension provides the decisive added value.
  • Securing advantages: If an in-house development strengthens your market position or makes your customers happier, then we will defend this solution.
  • Modern Development: We use the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to create individuality without contaminating the digital core.

Partnerships and Market Trends 2026

The market is responding to this need for expert knowledge. Large consulting firms like KPMG and Deloitte are heavily investing in strategic alliances to bridge the gap between standard software and customized AI solutions. As a partner in the SAP Open Ecosystem, we offer you precisely this flexibility: the security of a global market leader combined with the personalized support your business requires.

Conclusion: Balance wins

Whether you're building your SAP systems from scratch or need a complex migration strategy, let's discuss your options together. The goal is a system that's stable enough for the future, yet flexible enough to reflect your unique strengths.

entry.title: Unlocking Transformational Value at SAP Sapphire 2026 Orlando
entry.content:
Unlocking Transformational Value at SAP Sapphire: Join the Exclusive ASUG Pre-Conference on the New SAP Services & Support Portfolio

SAP is entering a new era of services and support—one that is simpler, more intelligent, and designed to help organizations move faster toward cloud transformation and AI-driven innovation. To help customers understand and fully leverage this new approach, ASUG and SAP are hosting an exclusive full-day pre-conference at SAP Sapphire:

“Unlocking Transformational Value: A Practical Introduction to the New SAP Services & Support Portfolio.”

This immersive event brings together SAP leaders, product experts, and customers to walk through the modernized SAP Services & Support portfolio, explain the new tiered Success Plans, and demonstrate how organizations can accelerate cloud adoption, improve operations, and drive measurable business outcomes. With breakfast, lunch, expert sessions, and an exclusive showfloor tour, the day provides both strategic insight and practical guidance for anyone responsible for SAP transformation. Whether you are planning a cloud migration, optimizing hybrid landscapes, or exploring how AI can transform operations, this session will help you understand how to maximize value from your SAP investment.
Register Now!

What You’ll Learn

During this full-day experience, participants will explore:

  • The new SAP Success Plans: Foundational, Advanced, and Max

  • How AI-enabled services improve productivity and automation

  • Ways to optimize SAP operations and governance

  • How Development Services and co-innovation accelerate innovation

  • How Application Management and Sovereign Cloud support modern enterprise requirements

You’ll leave with practical guidance for building your SAP success strategy for 2026 and beyond. Register Now!

Full Agenda May 11 | Orlando

8:30–9:00 AM — Welcome

Welcome & Setting the Stage

Geoff Scott, CEO and Chief Community Champion, ASUG

Kick off the day with ASUG and SAP leadership as they outline why SAP simplified its Services & Support portfolio. Learn what customers asked for and how the new model supports cloud adoption, AI-driven innovation, and predictable, business-aligned outcomes.

9:00–9:45 AM — Keynote Session

The New SAP Services & Support Portfolio: Simpler, Smarter, Predictable

Speakers:
Thomas Pfiester, Head of Customer Engagement & Adoption and Member of the Extended Board of SAP SE
Stefan Steinle, EVP and Head Global Customer Support, SAP
Uwe Grigoleit, SVP, Head of SAP Customer Evolution and Portfolio

Discover the structure and strategy behind SAP’s new Services & Support portfolio. This keynote introduces SAP Success Plans (Foundational, Advanced, and Max) alongside Development Services, Application Management, Professional Services, and SAP Sovereign Cloud. Learn how AI-enabled services simplify operations, increase predictability, and accelerate adoption, innovation, and business value.

9:45–9:55 AM — Break

9:55–10:25 AM — Success Plan Deep Dive

Foundational for Smart Adoption and Enablement

Speakers:
Rohan Patel, Global Head of Support, SAP Procurement
Jens Bernotat, Global Vice President Strategy, Portfolio, Global Customer Support, SAP

Learn what’s included in the SAP Foundational Success Plan, including SAP Enterprise Support, digital guidance, SAP Cloud ALM, and best practices. This session highlights how organizations can onboard faster, maintain system stability, and continuously improve operations across their SAP cloud landscape.

10:25–11:00 AM — Spotlight Session

Transforming the Cloud Journey with SAP Cloud ALM and Next-Generation Agents

Speakers:
Björn Braemer, SVP & Program Executive, Customer Evolution Program, SAP
Thorsten Spihlmann, Head of Business Development for Data Transformation, SAP

SAP Cloud ALM is evolving into a Transformation Execution Platform, helping organizations accelerate cloud transformation. Discover how an enhanced AI toolchain and agentic AI capabilities support configuration, migration, data preparation, and automated testing—reducing manual effort and enabling faster, smarter transformation journeys.

11:00–11:45 AM — Success Plan Deep Dive

Advanced for Guided, Predictable Transformation

Speakers:
Peter Roberts, SVP & Chief Product Officer, Advanced Success Plan, SAP

The Advanced Success Plan provides expert-guided engagement for complex transformation programs. Learn how tailored guidance, architectural reviews, readiness checks, and proactive insights help ensure predictable adoption and business-aligned outcomes across regions and teams.

11:45 AM–12:45 PM — Lunch Break

12:45–1:30 PM — Success Plan Deep Dive

Max for Strategic Partnership and Business-Critical Success

Speakers:
Sebastien Denes, Global Vice President, Chief Product Officer Max Success Plan, SAP

Explore SAP’s highest level of engagement with the Max Success Plan. Learn how dedicated experts, proactive governance, AI-driven insights, and mission-critical operations support help enterprises protect business continuity, accelerate innovation, and drive strategic outcomes at scale.

1:30–2:15 PM — Portfolio Extension Deep Dive

Co-Innovating with SAP Development Services: From AI Ideas to Impact

Speakers:
Sindhu Gangadharan, Head, Customer Innovation Services; MD, SAP Labs India

Discover how SAP Development Services partners with customers to build tailored solutions—from generative AI use cases to agentic AI scenarios. This session includes a real-world example demonstrating how custom AI innovation improved efficiency, decision-making, and measurable business outcomes.

2:15–3:00 PM — Portfolio Extension Deep Dive

Next Generation Application Management for Predictive, Intelligent, and Automated Operations

Speakers:
Lalit Patil, EVP & Chief Technology Officer, SAP
Vinay Hiriganahalli, SVP, Head of Cloud Application Services, SAP

Learn how SAP Application Management is transforming operations from reactive support to predictive, AI-driven optimization. Through artificial intelligence, machine learning, and cognitive automation, organizations can move beyond issue resolution toward proactive performance management and intelligent operations.

3:00–3:15 PM — Break

3:15–3:45 PM — Portfolio Extension Deep Dive

Secure, Compliant Innovation for Regulated Industries and Public Sector

Peter Keller, VP, Sovereign Cloud Architecture, SAP

Explore how SAP Sovereign Cloud helps organizations meet strict data residency, compliance, and security requirements while still accelerating innovation. The session covers architecture, use cases, and practical steps for adopting sovereign cloud models across regulated industries and the public sector.

3:45–4:30 PM — Strategy Session

Build Your SAP Success Strategy: Practical Guidance and Roadmap 2026

Uwe Grigoleit, SVP, Head of SAP Customer Evolution and Portfolio, SAP

And to finish the day: Experience the SAP Formula 1 racing simulator and connect with SAP experts in an interactive setting. Register Now!

Participants will gain:
